title: quercus frainetto var. aurea
intro: Quercus frainetto var. aurea is a rare, golden-leaved variant of the Quercus frainetto tree, commonly found in Eastern Europe and the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Quercus frainetto var. aurea include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system, while also exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. Quercus frainetto var. aurea can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of various conditions.
